key_points: To bring the AIwashing project from its current researchgrade state to a publicationready system, it makes sense to execute the transformation in several small iterations rather than attempting a oneoff overhaul.  Each iteration applies the framework you designed (data validation, observability, prompt engineering, error handling, testing, deployment, documentation, cost control) to a manageable set of features and issues.  Below is a suggested multiiteration roadmap.  It draws on the current codebase (as documented in the repository README) and the tasks laid out in your delivery plan , integrates the methodological requirements from your paper, and considers the option of using the OpenAI API for classification.
⬇️ Starting Point: Current State

The project already has a clearly defined pipeline: extract AIrelated sentences from 10K filings, encode labelled sentences with MPNet, compute centroids, classify sentences, aggregate to firmyear counts, merge with patents/compustat, and run regressions .

Scripts exist to embed sentences (embed_labeled_sentences_mpnet.py), compute centroids (compute_centroids_mpnet.py), classify sentences (classify_all_ai_sentences.py), aggregate counts (aggregate_classification_counts.py) and run evaluations .
The classifier uses a twostage approach: a heuristicsbased irrelevance gate followed by MPNet centroid scoring, optionally with lexical boosts .

The delivery plan notes that the current extraction splits sentences across page boundaries; labelled data must be expanded; interrater reliability (IRR) must be measured; crosswalks must be updated; multiple regressions must be run; and a final package delivered .
Given this context, the roadmap focuses on iterative improvements along both functionality and productionreadiness dimensions.
🧭 Iteration 0 – Stabilise the Extraction & Packaging
Goal: get a working baseline pipeline (even if rough) that produces complete AI sentences and can be run endtoend on a small sample.
Area
Actions in Iteration 0
Why
Data extraction & validation

Fix the sentencefragment issue described in Stage 0 by merging pagesplit fragments . Write a postprocessing function to detect broken sentences (e.g., page numbers and missing capitals) and reconstruct them by concatenating the preceding and following fragments. Validate that each sentence starts with a capital letter and ends with a period. Run this fix on a small sample of filings (e.g., 2024) and verify improved completeness.
Incomplete sentences reduce AI count accuracy. Fixing this ensures that classification operates on real sentences, not fragments.
Code organization

Convert the repo into a proper Python package (e.g., src/semantic_ai_washing), adopt a pyproject.toml, and restructure modules logically (data, classification, aggregation, analysis). Add __init__.py files and update imports accordingly.
Packaging is the foundation for subsequent tooling (tests, docs, CI).
Observability & logging
Replace print statements with structured logging (e.g., Python logging with JSON formatter). Include at least request ID, sentence ID, and stage.
Early instrumentation allows easier debugging when running the pipeline on multiple years later.
Testing & CI

Set up a minimal test suite with pytest. Start with smoke tests: verify that the extraction returns nonempty sentences; ensure classify_sentence returns one of the three labels. Configure a GitHub Actions CI workflow to run tests and linting on pushes.
An early CI baseline catches regressions as you iterate.
Documentation
Create or update a README section outlining installation, running the extraction script, and the purpose of the package. Note current limitations (e.g. classification is MPNetonly).
Documentation prevents confusion for collaborators and future you.

✅ Outcome: by the end of Iteration 0, you should be able to run the extraction and classification scripts on a sample year (e.g. 2024) and produce labeled *_classified.txt files with complete sentences.  The project is now packaged and minimally observable/testable.
🧭 Iteration 1 – Expand Training Data & Retrain MPNet Centroids
Goal: improve classification accuracy and reliability, and begin implementing productiongrade practices.
Actions in Iteration 1
Training data & IRR

Follow the Stage 1 and Stage 2 tasks: expand the labelled set by handlabelling additional AI sentences for a pilot sample (≥200 sentences, removing duplicates) . Use a second rater to label a stratified subset, compute Cohen’s kappa and ensure κ≥0.6 (substantial agreement) . Resolve disagreements and finalize the labeled set.
A larger and reliable labelled dataset improves centroids and ensures your rubric is welldefined.
Reembed & recompute centroids

Run embed_labeled_sentences_mpnet.py and compute_centroids_mpnet.py to generate new embeddings and centroids based on the expanded dataset . Point src/core/classify.py to the new centroids and verify classification still works.
Better centroids yield better classification.
Batch classification on limited years

Run classify_all_ai_sentences.py for a few years (e.g., 2021–2024) using the twostage classifier with recommended parameters (--two-stage --rule-boosts --tau 0.07 --eps-irr 0.03 --min-tokens 6) . Evaluate accuracy on a heldout set using evaluate_classifier_on_held_out.py and aim for ≥80% accuracy .
Validates that the improved classifier scales to multiple years.
gates: The classifier uses a twostage approach: a heuristicsbased irrelevance gate followed by MPNet centroid scoring, optionally with lexical boosts .
risks: Structure the transformation into iterative cycles.  Each cycle implements improvements across the framework’s eight dimensions, reduces risk by validating early, and brings the code closer to productionreadiness.
